mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals weakening labor demand and future earnings contraction. Equity investors reprice risk downward in anticipation of lower corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where PAYEMS declines month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5%, monetary tightening reduces liquidity available for equity risk-taking. Higher discount rates compress equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices immediately.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 1.69%, below trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with expanding housing starts (HOUST) signals robust consumer demand and construction activity. This dual demand expansion supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 497559.0 million dollars, HOUST at 1319.0 thousand units
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST are rising, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below its 3-month moving average, signaling supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization. This constrains corporate output and profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where INDPRO falls below 3-month MA, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.5% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) declines simultaneously, signaling labor market deterioration. This joint signal indicates weakening consumer purchasing power and future earnings risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 3.9%, below trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of instances where both UNRATE > 4.5% and PAYEMS declines, or interaction Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2%, indicating real rates are negative. This monetary accommodation supports equity risk appetite and asset price inflation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances where both conditions are met, or interaction Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth decelerates below 1.5% annualized rate, signaling weakening aggregate demand and future earnings contraction. Equity investors reduce growth expectations and compress valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 17385.831 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where GDPC1 growth < 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) expands above its 6-month moving average while unemployment (UNRATE) remains below 4%, signaling robust supply-side capacity and tight labor markets. This productivity-employment combination supports corporate margin expansion and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where both conditions are met, or interaction Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.
